free_text: Systematic study of low-energy incomplete-fusion
dynamics in the 16O + 148Nd system: Role of target
deformation
Central University of Jharkhand
Sum of production cross sections in figs.
15UD Pelletron accelerator
The targets of 14Nd having thickness ~100-300 ug/cm2
were prepared vacuum evaporation technique.
The target material was deposited on an Al backing of
thickness ~1.2-1.7 mg/cm2 were prepared by rolling
technique.
The HPGe detector of 100 cm3 active volume
coupled to a PC through CAMAC was calibrated using
standard 152Eu gamma-source.
The energy resolution of detector was ~2.3 keV for
1408 keV gamma-ray of 152Eu source.
The stack was irradiated for 10 hrs.
The beam current was ~2-3 pnA during the irradiation of
targets.
Total uncertainty including statistical error
Target nonuniformity and thickness
measurement                        (~<3%)
Efficiency calibration of detector   (~<5%)
Fluctuations of beam current (~<6%)
Dead time                   (<10%)
Straggling effect of projectile    (<2%)
D. Dutta
On. REFERENCE: J,IPA,57,552,2019 added.
